name: spec-extraction-from-requirements
description: Validate that the skill extracts complete, testable specifications from a natural language requirements document using structured formats.
input:
Extract testable specifications from this requirements document:

## User Registration
- Users must register with email and password.
- Email must be unique across the system.
- Password must be at least 8 characters with one uppercase, one lowercase, and one number.
- After registration, a verification email is sent.
- Unverified accounts cannot access protected resources.
- Verification links expire after 24 hours.
- Users can request a new verification email, but not more than 3 times per hour.

Provide specifications in GIVEN-WHEN-THEN format with IDs and classification.

assertions.description: Must use the GIVEN-WHEN-THEN format for specifications, as this is the standard format for testable behavioral specs.
Must extract the email uniqueness constraint as a distinct specification, since it requires a specific database-level check.
Must extract the verification link expiry as a testable time-bound specification, not just a narrative description.
Must extract the 3-per-hour rate limit on verification email requests as a non-functional specification.
Must extract the access restriction on unverified accounts as a security specification with explicit test criteria.

name: verification-workflow
description: Validate that the skill can verify an implementation against specifications and identify gaps.

assertions.description: Must identify SPEC-003 as not satisfied because there is no setQuantity method that handles setting quantity to 0.
Must identify SPEC-005 as not satisfied because there is no checkout method that validates cart emptiness.
Must confirm SPEC-001 is satisfied by the addItem method with quantity tracking.
Must confirm SPEC-004 is satisfied by the existing item check in addItem that increments quantity.
Must explicitly identify specification gaps and recommend implementation changes to close them.
